name: V-Model Regulatory Evidence Pipeline
description: Serial V-model with peer routing: RA defines requirements, Developer builds, QA verifies, RA accepts. No manager in the routing chain -- agents route directly to each other via teamMembers.

REQUIREMENTS_DEFINITION.name: Requirements Definition
REQUIREMENTS_DEFINITION.description: RA writes the initial minimal requirement for a CLI application.
REQUIREMENTS_DEFINITION.prompt:
You are the Requirements Analyst. Write or update the requirements specification for the task described below.

Work in your workspace/project directory:
1. Pull latest from origin (there may be prior work from earlier tasks)
2. Create or update docs/requirements-specification.md with a section for the requirement described in the task details. Include the REQ-HCDP-XXX identifier
3. Create or update docs/acceptance-criteria-checklist.md with acceptance criteria for this requirement
4. Push to origin and report completion

Keep changes focused on the single requirement in this task.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the requirements definition work just completed, were ALL of the following accomplished: (1) requirements-specification.md created or updated with the REQ-HCDP-XXX identifier and source annotation, (2) acceptance-criteria-checklist.md created or updated, and (3) changes committed and pushed to origin? Answer with a single word: true or false.

onEntryCommands.reason: Fetch latest remote tracking references to see changes pushed by other agents.
Switch to the {{branch}} branch. If it does not exist locally, create it with: git checkout -b {{branch}}. If uncommitted changes block checkout, use: git checkout -f {{branch}}.

Force-sync local branch to match origin/{{branch}}. This ensures you start with the latest work from other agents. If origin/{{branch}} does not exist yet (fresh repo, first task), skip this step and continue.

onExitCommands.reason: Stage all new and modified files including untracked files.
Commit all staged changes. If there is nothing new to commit, use the --allow-empty flag.
Capture the commit SHA for downstream traceability.
Push all local commits to origin so subsequent agents can pull this work. This is critical -- if this fails, downstream agents will not see your changes. Retry or diagnose the failure.

IMPLEMENTING.name: Implementation
IMPLEMENTING.description: Developer builds the minimal CLI app per the requirement.
IMPLEMENTING.prompt:
You are the Developer. Implement the requirement described in the task details.

Work in your workspace/project directory:
1. Pull latest from origin (there may be prior work from earlier tasks)
2. Read docs/requirements-specification.md to understand the requirement
3. Create or update source files in src/
4. Create or update test files in tests/
5. Create test fixtures in tests/fixtures/ if needed
6. If package.json does not exist, create it (name: hcdp-validate) with tsconfig.json and jest.config.js
7. Run npm install && npx jest -- all tests must pass
8. Create or update evidence/developer-traceability.md mapping each REQ to its implementation commits and files
9. Commit with a message that includes the REQ-HCDP-XXX identifier (e.g. 'implement REQ-HCDP-001: add CLI skeleton') and push to origin, report completion with the commit hash

Keep changes focused on this task's requirement.

TypeScript strict mode: In catch blocks, the error variable is type 'unknown'. Always use 'catch (err: unknown)' and cast before accessing properties, e.g. '(err as Error).message' or guard with 'if (err instanceof Error)'.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the implementation work just completed, did ALL of the following succeed: (1) source files created or updated, (2) test files created or updated, (3) all tests pass when running npx jest, (4) developer-traceability.md updated, and (5) changes committed with the REQ-HCDP-XXX identifier in the commit message and pushed to origin? Answer with a single word: true or false.

onEntryCommands.reason: Fetch latest remote tracking references to see changes pushed by other agents.
Switch to the {{branch}} branch. If it does not exist locally, create it with: git checkout -b {{branch}}. If uncommitted changes block checkout, use: git checkout -f {{branch}}.
Force-sync local branch to match origin/{{branch}}. This ensures you start with the latest code including requirements docs from the RA. If origin/{{branch}} does not exist, skip.

onExitCommands.reason: Stage all new and modified files including untracked files.
Commit all staged changes. If there is nothing new to commit, use the --allow-empty flag.
Capture the commit SHA for downstream traceability.
Push all local commits to origin so QA can pull and verify. This is critical -- if this fails, QA will not see your implementation. Retry or diagnose the failure.

VERIFICATION.name: QA Verification
VERIFICATION.description: QA verifies the developer's implementation against the requirements.
VERIFICATION.prompt:
You are QA. Verify the implementation for this task's requirement in your workspace/project directory.

IMPORTANT: The workflow engine already ran `git log --oneline --grep='REQ-HCDP-'` before you started and captured the result as reqCommits. Do NOT re-run this command. Reference the captured output to confirm traceability.

1. Check the reqCommits captured value -- it must contain at least one commit line referencing the requirement. If empty, record a FAIL for traceability.
2. Record the verified commit: run `git rev-parse HEAD` and save the hash
3. Run tests: npm install && npx jest
4. Create or update evidence/traceability-matrix.md with a row for this requirement:
   | Requirement | Implementation Commit | Tests Pass | Verified Commit | Status |
5. Create or update evidence/verification-report.md with the verified commit hash and verdict (PASS or FAIL)
6. If PASS, merge to main: git checkout main && git merge --no-ff -m 'QA verified' && git push origin main
7. Push evidence to origin and report completion with the commit hash

When updating verification, validation, or traceability reports, preserve all existing entries from prior tasks. Append new rows or sections -- do not overwrite past evidence.

You produce evidence only. You do NOT accept -- that is the RA's job.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the QA verification work just completed, did ALL of the following pass: (1) the captured reqCommits value is non-empty (at least one commit line references a REQ-HCDP-XXX identifier), (2) all unit tests passed, and (3) the verification report verdict is PASS? Answer with a single word: true or false.

onEntryCommands.reason: Fetch latest remote tracking references to see the developer's pushed implementation.
Switch to the {{branch}} branch. If it does not exist locally, create it with: git checkout -b {{branch}}. If uncommitted changes block checkout, use: git checkout -f {{branch}}.
Force-sync local branch to match origin/{{branch}}. This ensures you are verifying exactly what the developer pushed, not stale local files.

Capture all commits whose message references a REQ-HCDP-XXX identifier. Output is stored in reqCommits for use by QA and the exit evaluation. Empty output means the developer did not annotate any commit with a requirement ID and is a traceability failure.

onExitCommands.reason: Stage all new and modified files including evidence documents.
Commit all staged changes including verification evidence. If there is nothing new to commit, use the --allow-empty flag.
Capture the commit SHA for downstream traceability.
Push verification evidence to origin so the RA can review it during acceptance. This is critical -- if this fails, the RA will not see QA evidence. Retry or diagnose the failure.

REWORK.name: Rework
REWORK.prompt:
QA found issues. Read the QA verification report in evidence/ and fix EACH gap listed.

TRACEABILITY FIX (if reqCommits was empty or missing a required REQ-HCDP-XXX ID):
  Do NOT amend or rebase existing commits. Instead add an empty traceability commit:
    git commit --allow-empty -m 'traceability: REQ-HCDP-XXX reference for {{taskId}}'
  Replace REQ-HCDP-XXX with the actual identifier from docs/requirements-specification.md.
  Then push: git push origin {{branch}}
  This is sufficient -- the next QA run will find it via git log --grep.

For all other QA failures: fix the code or tests, run npx jest to confirm all tests pass, then push.

TypeScript strict mode: In catch blocks, the error variable is type 'unknown'. Always use 'catch (err: unknown)' and cast before accessing properties, e.g. '(err as Error).message' or guard with 'if (err instanceof Error)'.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the rework just completed, did ALL of the following succeed: (1) all QA-identified issues addressed, (2) all tests pass when running npx jest, (3) at least one commit in the git log references a REQ-HCDP-XXX identifier (check the reworkReqCommits captured value -- it must be non-empty), and (4) changes committed and pushed to origin? Answer with a single word: true or false.

onEntryCommands.reason: Fetch latest remote tracking references to see QA's verification evidence.
Switch to the {{branch}} branch. If it does not exist locally, create it with: git checkout -b {{branch}}. If uncommitted changes block checkout, use: git checkout -f {{branch}}.
Force-sync to origin/{{branch}} so you see QA's failure evidence and the current code state.
Check whether any commit already references a REQ-HCDP-XXX identifier before rework begins. Developer uses this to know if a traceability commit is needed.

onExitCommands.reason: Stage all rework changes.
Commit rework fixes. If there is nothing new to commit, use the --allow-empty flag.
Capture the commit SHA for downstream traceability.
Push rework fixes to origin so QA can re-verify. This is critical.

ACCEPTANCE.name: Requirements Acceptance
ACCEPTANCE.description: RA reviews QA evidence and issues final accept/reject verdict.
ACCEPTANCE.prompt:
You are the RA performing final acceptance for this task's requirement in your workspace/project directory.

1. Pull latest from origin (main branch -- QA merged verified code here)
2. Read evidence/verification-report.md and evidence/traceability-matrix.md
3. Note the Verified Commit hash from the QA report
4. Check traceability: run `git log --oneline | grep REQ-HCDP` to confirm commits reference this requirement
5. Create or update evidence/acceptance-verdict.md with a row for this requirement:

   | Requirement | Criteria Met | Evidence | Verdict |

   Include the verified commit hash and overall verdict (ACCEPTED / REJECTED)

6. Push and report completion.

If QA verdict was PASS and at least one commit references the requirement identifier, the verdict should be ACCEPTED.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the RA acceptance work just completed, did the Requirements Analyst issue an ACCEPTED verdict for all requirements under review? Answer with a single word: true or false.

onEntryCommands.reason: Fetch latest remote tracking references to see QA's merged and verified code.
Switch to the {{branch}} branch. If it does not exist locally, create it with: git checkout -b {{branch}}. If uncommitted changes block checkout, use: git checkout -f {{branch}}.
Force-sync to origin/{{branch}} to ensure you see QA-verified code and evidence.

onExitCommands.reason: Stage acceptance verdict and any updated evidence.
Commit acceptance verdict. If there is nothing new to commit, use the --allow-empty flag.
Capture the commit SHA for audit trail.
Push acceptance evidence to origin. This is critical for audit trail.

REWORK_ACCEPTANCE.name: Rework (Acceptance)
REWORK_ACCEPTANCE.prompt: RA rejected the delivery. Fix the listed gaps, push, and report completion.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the acceptance rework just completed, did ALL of the following succeed: (1) all RA-identified rejection issues addressed, (2) all tests pass, and (3) changes committed and pushed to origin? Answer with a single word: true or false.

onEntryCommands.reason: Fetch latest remote tracking references to see RA's rejection feedback.
Switch to the {{branch}} branch. If it does not exist locally, create it with: git checkout -b {{branch}}. If uncommitted changes block checkout, use: git checkout -f {{branch}}.
Force-sync to origin/{{branch}} to see the RA's rejection evidence and current code.

onExitCommands.reason: Stage all rework changes.
Commit rework fixes. If there is nothing new to commit, use the --allow-empty flag.
Capture the commit SHA for downstream traceability.
Push rework fixes to origin so QA can re-verify. This is critical.

DONE.name: Complete
DONE.description: V-model complete. Terminal state -- the completing agent cleans up locally.

ESCALATED.name: Escalated
ESCALATED.description: Failed after retries. Terminal state -- the failing agent logs escalation.
